description: Indulge in a classic American steakhouse experience right at home with this exquisite New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter recipe. This dish features perfectly seared, tender strip steak infused with a rich, aromatic garlic herb butter, delivering an unforgettable flavor in every bite. It is surprisingly simple to master, making it ideal for a special dinner or a weeknight treat.

ingredients.notes: or high smoke point oil like avocado oil

instructions.title: Prepare the Steaks
instructions.text: Remove the New York Strip steaks from the refrigerator at least 30-60 minutes before cooking to allow them to come closer to room temperature. Pat them completely dry with paper towels. This is crucial for achieving a good sear.

instructions.title: Season Generously
instructions.text: Season both sides and the edges of each steak generously with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper. Do not be shy with the seasoning; a thick steak can handle a good amount.

instructions.title: Heat the Skillet
instructions.text: Place a heavy-bottomed cast iron skillet over high heat for 5-7 minutes until it is smoking hot. Add the olive oil to the hot skillet, ensuring it coats the bottom evenly.

instructions.title: Sear the Steaks
instructions.text: Carefully place the seasoned steaks in the hot skillet. Sear for 2-3 minutes per side until a deep brown crust forms. For medium-rare, aim for an internal temperature of 130-135°F (54-57°C). Adjust searing time for desired doneness.

instructions.title: Add Garlic Butter and Baste
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to medium-low. Add the cold cubed butter, minced garlic, rosemary, and thyme to the skillet. Once the butter is melted, tilt the pan slightly and use a spoon to continuously baste the steaks with the flavorful garlic herb butter for 1-2 minutes. This infuses the steak with incredible flavor.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the steaks from the skillet and transfer them to a cutting board. Tent loosely with foil and let them rest for at least 10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, ensuring a tender and juicy steak. Slice against the grain and pour any remaining pan drippings and garlic butter over the top before serving.

tags: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter
tips: Always bring your steak to room temperature before cooking. This ensures a more even cook from edge to edge.
A screaming hot cast iron skillet is essential for developing that perfect, crispy crust on your strip steak.
Do not skip the resting step! Resting allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb juices, resulting in a significantly juicier steak.
article:
New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter: The Ultimate American Dinner Recipe

There are few culinary experiences as satisfying as a perfectly cooked steak. And when it comes to classic American dinner recipes, the New York Strip Steak holds a special place. But what transforms a great steak into an unforgettable one? A luscious, aromatic garlic butter finish. This guide will walk you through creating a restaurant-quality New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter right in your own kitchen, guaranteed to impress.

## What is New York Strip Steak?

The New York Strip, also known as a Kansas City Strip, Ambassador Steak, or even a Club Steak, is a cut from the short loin of a cow. It’s located just behind the ribs, a muscle that doesn't do a lot of work, which contributes to its tenderness. Characterized by its fine, tender texture and robust, beefy flavor, the New York Strip is highly prized by steak enthusiasts. It typically has a good amount of marbling (intramuscular fat), which melts during cooking, infusing the meat with incredible juiciness and flavor. Often sold boneless, but sometimes bone-in, its distinct rectangular shape and a band of fat along one side make it easily recognizable. It strikes a perfect balance between the lean tenderness of a filet mignon and the intense marbling of a ribeye, making it an ideal canvas for rich flavors like garlic butter.

## Why You'll Love This Garlic Butter Steak Recipe

Imagine a steak that’s perfectly seared to a deep, mahogany crust, revealing a tender, juicy interior with every slice. Now imagine that steak bathed in a fragrant, bubbling garlic butter sauce infused with fresh herbs. That’s the magic of this New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter recipe. You'll love it because it’s surprisingly simple to execute yet yields incredibly impressive results. This isn't just a meal; it’s an experience. It’s a quintessential American dinner recipe that brings gourmet dining home, allowing you to control the quality of ingredients and the exact doneness of your steak. The rich, savory notes of the beef are perfectly complemented by the pungent garlic and herbaceous butter, creating a symphony of flavors that will have everyone asking for seconds. It’s ideal for a special occasion, a romantic dinner, or simply elevating a weeknight meal into something extraordinary.

## Ingredients for Perfect New York Strip Steak

Achieving steak perfection starts with quality ingredients. Here’s what you’ll need to create your magnificent New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter:

*   **New York Strip Steaks:** Two 1.25 to 1.5-inch thick boneless New York Strip steaks, about 12-16 oz each. Choose steaks with good marbling.
*   **Salt:** 1-2 teaspoons coarse sea salt or kosher salt. Don't be shy; steak needs generous seasoning.
*   **Black Pepper:** 1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper.
*   **Olive Oil:** 1-2 tablespoons high-smoke point olive oil (extra virgin is fine, but regular olive oil is often better for high heat).
*   **Unsalted Butter:** 4-6 tablespoons (½ stick) cold unsalted butter, cut into pats. Unsalted allows you to control the saltiness.
*   **Garlic:** 4-6 cloves fresh garlic, smashed and peeled. You can also thinly slice them.
*   **Fresh Herbs:** 2-3 sprigs fresh rosemary and/or thyme. These herbs infuse the butter with incredible aroma.
*   **Optional:** A small splash of Worcestershire sauce for added umami, or a squeeze of lemon juice at the end for brightness.

## Step-by-Step Guide to Cooking Strip Steak

Mastering the New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is easier than you think. Follow these steps for a flawless result:

1.  **Prep the Steaks:** Remove steaks from the refrigerator 30-60 minutes before cooking to allow them to come closer to room temperature. This promotes more even cooking. Pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels – this is crucial for a good sear. Season generously all over with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
2.  **Heat the Pan:** Place a heavy-bottomed skillet (cast iron is ideal) over high heat until smoking lightly, about 3-5 minutes. Add the olive oil and swirl to coat the bottom.
3.  **Sear the Steaks:** Carefully place the seasoned steaks into the hot pan. Sear undisturbed for 2-3 minutes per side until a deep, golden-brown crust forms. For a 1.5-inch thick steak, you might also sear the edges for about 30-60 seconds each.
4.  **Add Butter and Aromatics:** Reduce the heat to medium-low. Add the cold butter pats, smashed garlic cloves, and herb sprigs to the pan.
5.  **Baste:** As the butter melts and sizzles, tilt the pan slightly and use a large spoon to continuously baste the melted garlic butter over the steaks. Continue basting for 2-4 minutes, or until the steaks reach your desired internal temperature.
6.  **Check Doneness:** Use an instant-read meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the steak (avoiding the bone, if present) to check for doneness.
    *   Rare: 120-125°F
    *   Medium-Rare: 130-135°F
    *   Medium: 135-140°F
    *   Medium-Well: 145-150°F
    *   Well-Done: 155°F+
    *   Remember, the temperature will rise by 5-10 degrees during resting. For medium-rare, remove at 125-130°F.
7.  **Rest the Steaks:** Transfer the New York Strip steaks to a cutting board. Tent loosely with foil and let them rest for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, ensuring a supremely juicy steak.
8.  **Serve:** Slice against the grain, spoon any remaining garlic butter from the pan over the top, and serve immediately.

## Tips for the Juiciest Garlic Butter Steak

Crafting the perfect New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter involves a few key techniques:

*   **Start at Room Temperature:** Letting your steak sit out for 30-60 minutes before cooking helps it cook more evenly from edge to center.
*   **Pat it Dry, Dry, Dry:** Moisture is the enemy of a good sear. Use paper towels to remove all surface moisture. This allows the Maillard reaction (browning) to occur, creating that delicious crust.
*   **High Heat for the Sear:** Don't be afraid of a smoking hot pan. The initial high heat is what creates that beautiful, flavorful crust.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Pan:** If cooking more than two steaks, cook them in batches. Overcrowding lowers the pan temperature and steams the meat instead of searing it.
*   **Use Cold Butter for Basting:** Adding cold butter to the pan helps regulate the temperature and prevents the butter from burning too quickly while basting.
*   **Baste Continuously:** The constant spooning of hot garlic butter over the steak not only infuses it with flavor but also helps cook the steak gently and evenly.
*   **Rest is Best:** This is arguably the most crucial step. Resting allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb the juices that have migrated to the center during cooking. Skipping this step results in a dry steak, regardless of how perfectly it was cooked.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

The rich flavor of New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter pairs wonderfully with a variety of sides, making it a versatile centerpiece for any American dinner recipe.

*   **Classic Comfort:** Creamy mashed potatoes, baked potato with all the fixings, or crispy roasted potatoes are always a hit.
*   **Green Companions:** Steamed or roasted asparagus, sautéed green beans with almonds, creamed spinach, or a simple fresh garden salad cut through the richness.
*   **Hearty Options:** Macaroni and cheese, risotto, or grilled corn on the cob provide satisfying complements.
*   **Wine Pairings:** For red wine lovers, a bold Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, or Zinfandel will stand up beautifully to the robust flavors of the steak. If you prefer white, a full-bodied Chardonnay could also work.
*   **Non-Alcoholic:** Iced tea, sparkling water with a lemon wedge, or a rich root beer can be excellent choices.

No matter how you serve it, this garlic butter steak is bound to be a memorable meal.

## Storage & Reheating Instructions

While it's unlikely you'll have leftovers of such a delicious New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter, here's how to store and reheat it properly if you do:

*   **Storage:** Allow any leftover steak to cool completely, then transfer it to an airtight container. Store in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.
*   **Freezing:** Cooked steak can be frozen, but it may lose some texture and tenderness upon thawing. Wrap individual slices tightly in plastic wrap, then in foil, and place in a freezer-safe bag. Freeze for up to 1 month. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator before reheating.
*   **Reheating:** The key to reheating steak is to do it gently to avoid drying it out.
    *   **Oven Method:** Preheat your oven to a low temperature (around 250-275°F or 120-135°C). Place the steak on a wire rack set over a baking sheet. Heat for 15-25 minutes, or until warmed through. A little extra garlic butter or beef broth can be added to the pan to help keep it moist.
    *   **Skillet Method:** Heat a skillet over medium-low heat with a small amount of oil or butter. Add the steak and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, just until warmed through. Don't let it sit too long, or it will continue to cook and dry out.
    *   **Avoid the Microwave:** While convenient, microwaves often unevenly heat and can make steak tough and rubbery.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: What's the best thickness for a New York Strip steak?**
A: For optimal searing and even cooking, a New York Strip steak that is 1.25 to 1.5 inches thick is generally recommended.

**Q: How do I know when my steak is done without cutting into it?**
A: An instant-read meat thermometer is your best friend. Insert it into the thickest part of the steak. For a medium-rare New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter, aim for an internal temperature of 130-135°F (54-57°C) before resting.

**Q: Can I use different herbs for the garlic butter?**
A: Absolutely! While rosemary and thyme are classic, you can experiment with other herbs like sage, oregano, or even a touch of fresh parsley for a different flavor profile.

**Q: Is it okay to use margarine instead of butter?**
A: For the best flavor and browning, unsalted butter is highly recommended. Margarine has a higher water content and different fat composition, which won't yield the same rich taste or beautiful golden crust.

**Q: Can I cook New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter on a grill?**
A: Yes, you can grill New York Strip steaks first, then transfer them to a pan to baste with the garlic butter, or simply brush the melted garlic butter over the steaks immediately after they come off the grill. This combines the smoky flavor of grilling with the richness of the butter.
